title: Inspect /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public for remaining Claude-defaulted paths that block a Claude-free Codex-led orchestration. Focus on scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh, scripts/lib/orchestrator-policy.sh, scripts/lib/build-agent-matrix.sh, scripts/harness/aggregate-tutti-votes...
summary_text: The remaining Claude-default blockers in the focused flow are concentrated in these files: 1. 4 core scripts to edit for the requested scope - [`scripts/lib/orchestrator-policy.sh`](...): - Default assist is still `claude` via `default_assist="claude"` at line 24 and final fallback at line 25 (`--default-assist` docs/options at 27–29). - Assist fallback/invariant logic enforces `main=claude -> assist=codex` (`if [[ "${resolved_main}" == "claude" ... ]]` around lines 165–207), which is hard-coded around the codex-led-with-claude architecture. - [`scripts/lib/build-agent-matrix.sh`](...): - Default `assist_provider` is `claude` (`assist_provider="claude"`) at line 9 and CLI fallback line 42. - Matrix construction still auto-adds Claude assist lanes by default (`if $assist_provider == "claude" then ... "claude-opus-assist"` at lines 348–350) and Claude Sonnet assist lanes in non-subscription context (355–359). - [`scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh`](...): - CLI/env default is `ASSIST_PROVIDER="${ASSIST_PROVIDER:-claude}"` line 13. - Documentation and policy defaults still present: `FUGUE_LOCAL_REQUIRE_CLAUDE_ASSIST` (line 77), `FUGUE_LOCAL_REQUIRE_CLAUDE_ASSIST_ON_COMPLEX` default `true` (78), `FUGUE_LOCAL_REQUIRE_BASELINE_TRIO` default `true` (82), and CLAUDE fallback logic driven by those flags (820–937, plus baseline/reporting outputs 1119–1120). - Matrix is launched with `--assist-provider "${ASSIST_PROVIDER}"` at line 605, so default flows stay Claude-assist unless explicitly changed. - [`scripts/harness/aggregate-tutti-votes.sh`](...): - Complex/direct assist gate is Claude-specific by design (`complex_claude_sub_required`, `required_claude_assist_gate`) at lines 68–85 and 123–149. - Baseline gate is still `codex+claude+glm` naming/expectation and fail reasons (lines 169–176, 919–938, 1120 in local orchestration summary consumers). 2. Tests that will need update to match a Claude-free default posture - [`tests/test-orchestrator-policy.sh`](...): - Default assertions still expect `assist=claude` when explicit values are empty (`--main "" --assist "" --claude-state ok` at 181–183). - Many fixtures still encode Claude-as-baseline-assist behavior for `degraded/normal` policy scenarios; at least lines 87–213 require re-baselining if assist default changes. - [`tests/test-route-task-handoff.sh`](...): - Hard-coded default in test environment: `DEFAULT_ASSIST_ORCHESTRATOR_PROVIDER="claude"` at lines 88, 154, 293. - [`tests/test-vote-handoff-simulation.sh`](...): - Hard-coded default in env setup: `DEFAULT_ASSIST_ORCHESTRATOR_PROVIDER="claude"` at lines 124 and 194. - [`tests/test-resolve-orchestration-context.sh`](...): - Test harness env still sets `DEFAULT_ASSIST_ORCHESTRATOR_PROVIDER=claude` (line 88) and this propagates into resolved defaults. If you want end-to-end removal of Claude-by-default beyond this scope (CI handoff path too), the next minimal set is: - `scripts/harness/resolve-orchestration-context.sh` lines 79 and 379. - `scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh` lines 172–173. - `scripts/gha24` where `--default-assist "claude"` is hard-coded for policy fallback around 279–281. I did not edit files.
